Football.title: Chelsea legend Eden Hazard’s wine tested: See which £25 bottle ‘crushed it’ and which left a sour 6/10 taste
Football.content:
FEW players have provided Chelsea fans with as many unforgettable memories as Eden Hazard.

Dazzling goals against Liverpool, a spectacular run and finish against West Ham, and the goal against Spurs that took Leicester to the Premier League title – he’s one of their best.





It’s safe to say he crushed it on the pitch… now he’s crushing grapes off it, with his own line of wines.

At SunSport, we’re becoming something of footballer-wine connoisseurs, having tried Lionel Messi’s vino among others.

So now, we have put Hazard’s £25 bottles to the test.



ROSE: NEGROMARO CABERNET IGP SALENTO: 8/10

This Rose definitely packs a punch.

The Negroamaro and Cabernet blend gives it a deep, bold character with dark berry and plum flavours coming through straight away.

It’s quite sharp on the palate with a strong, punchy finish (a character of Hazard’s playstyle), so not the smoothest to drink, but it certainly makes an impression.

It would pair brilliantly with some antipasti or fish, or be absolutely class if, like me, you like a bit of wine on a night out.



A powerful rose that stands out for its intensity.

WHITE: PINOT GRIGIO MALVASIA BIANCA IGP PUGLIA: 10/10

This was my favourite of the three; the Pinot Grigio was spectacular.

It’s a really crisp, refreshing white with bright citrus and green pear flavours, and a subtle floral note from the Malvasia.

The blend makes it light, smooth and very easy to drink.



Crisp like his incisive dribbling, it’s sharp, clean and leaves a really fresh finish – I can imagine this would go down a treat with something a little more spicy.

RED: ROSSO IGP SALENTO: 6/10

Most of my experience with red wine comes during the Eucharist at church, so trying this without ‘the body of Christ’ was a little different.

Maybe I’m just not cultured enough as a wine drinker, but this was my least favourite of the three.



That said, I can still appreciate it was packed full of flavour, and perhaps would’ve been better paired with some grub.

It’s the kind of red that feels made for food, and I think it would pair perfectly with something like steak and chips.

DOES SUNSPORT RECOMMEND?

Absolutely.

If you’re looking for something fun and football-themed to drink, Hazard’s wine is one to watch.



If you’re going on a trashy night out and just looking to get hammered, maybe it’s not the one for you at its £25 price point.

Even if you’re hosting a classy dinner, this will provide a fun talking point, where you can get stuck into the real action of why he’s better than Mo Salah and should be remembered as such…

The wine is delicious, and if you’re opting for one bottle, the Pinot Grigio was definitely a favourite here.

Watch the video at the top of this article and you’ll get a better taste for our thoughts…

The Chelsea Blueprint on YouTube Calling all Chelsea fans - we have a brand new YouTube channel! The Sun’s Chelsea YouTube channel will be your go-to destination for the latest news, transfer updates, match previews, post-match analysis, exclusive interviews, fan reactions, and breaking stories from Stamford Bridge. Subscribe to The Chelsea Blueprint on YouTube now

WHAT IS WINE OF THE CHAMPIONS?

Wine of the Champions Founder Fabio Cordella exclusively told SunSport: “My family and I produce the wine in Italy.



“This May will mark 10 years since I had the idea to build my dream team with wine bottles, and every player is in partnership with me to produce their bottles.

“For Eden Hazard, it’s his first time as a seller. We started trying the wine with different grapes directly from the barrels so we could understand, between us, what he likes, and then we chose the grapes.

“The project is that the players and I choose exactly what they like, and every label is different, which is nice for the customers.

“Some other sellers market their product as a private label, but they use the same wine and just change the label.

“With Wine of the Champions, we start the process right from the beginning.



“The players choose the grapes, and choose whether to make a blend or to use just the pure grapes to make their wines.

“Eden was involved just as much as the other players I have worked with. He was around for the wine tasting and testing, and he will be at the Vinitaly wine fair in April in Verona.

He laughed, “They are the winemakers without selling it themselves!”

Eden Hazard isn’t the only footballer that Cordella has worked with; he has produced wine for several stars.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

Ronaldinho, Wesley Sneijder, Ivan Zamorano, Julio Cesar, John Terry, Cafu, Roberto Carlos and others have all made wine labels as part of the Wine of the Champions collection.

Cordella’s goal is to get a team of 27-28 football legends who can come together in a team from time to time and take part in charity events and shows to share their passions and talents to generations to come.

Football.title: Man Utd legend reveals management nightmare after being told star player has left just an HOUR before kick-off
Football.content:
STEVE BRUCE has revealed he was stunned in his first managerial role – after including Dean Saunders in the starting line-up only for him to be SOLD before kick-off.

The three-time Championship promotion winner, 65, headed into the dugout for the first time as player-boss at Sheffield United in December 1998.





But ahead of facing Bristol City, Bruce found himself in a nightmare position and had to throw his pre-match plans out of the window.

The Manchester United legend was ready to task Saunders with firing the Blades to all three points.

And he was left in absolute shock when the striker revealed he had just been given the green light to complete a move to Portuguese giants Benfica.

Bruce told talkSPORT: “We’re playing Bristol City away and he’s due to play. I haven’t played for a few months and I’m the player-manager.

“I could hear him walking down the tunnel, walking down the hotel and this was about an hour [before leaving].

“He was whistling away, singing away and I said, ‘Dean, we’re not leaving for another hour, why have you got your bags?’

“He said, ‘Nobody told you?’ I said, ‘Told me what?’ He said, ‘I’ve just signed for Benfica’. I said, ‘What? Just signed for Benfica?’ He said, ‘Yes, I’m on the half past three from Heathrow’.

“He says, ‘Thanks ever so much Steve, it’s a pleasure to meet you’, and walks out through the door and I thought, ‘Who am I going to call?’



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets

“That was my first job in management and I thought, ‘Wow, wow, what do I do about this?’ And I found it very, very difficult I have to tell you.”

Saunders, who had already notched ten goals in 23 appearances under Bruce, sealed his switch to Benfica three days later.

And Sheffield United went on to suffer a 2-0 defeat at Ashton Gate, courtesy of a Mickey Bell penalty and Ade Akinbiyi adding his name to the scoresheet.



Bruce has gone on to take charge of more than 1,000 games – thanks to spells in charge of the likes of Wigan, Birmingham, Sunderland, Hull, Aston Villa and Newcastle.

A whopping 477 of those showdowns came in the Premier League, leading to 133 wins.

But Bruce has been without a managerial job since being sacked by Blackpool last October.

Football.title: Promising footballer, 17, died after suffering heart attack during match as staff missed signs that could have saved him
Football.content:
FOOTY TRAGEDY Promising footballer, 17, died after suffering heart attack during match as staff missed signs that could have saved him

A PROMISING young footballer died after suffering a heart attack on the pitch as a coroner warns staff missed signs that could have saved him.

Adam Ankers, 17, was playing for the Chairboys’ Foundation Under-19 team in 2024 when he suffered a sudden cardiac arrest.



Towards the end of the second half the youngster shouted “my chest is tight” before losing consciousness.



Paramedics were scrambled to the scene and Adam was rushed to hospital but he sadly died days later.

Following an inquest into the tragedy, a coroner has warned that Adam’s death had been “more than minimally” contributed to by a failure to identify that he was in cardiac arrest.

Because of this no one was instructed to perform CPR or use a defibrillator in the crucial minutes before paramedics arrived.

Assistant coroner at West London Coroner’s Court, Valerie Charbit, said: “There was a missed opportunity to deliver basic life support for Adam.

“Agonal breathing and cardiac arrest were not identified by the 999-call handler or those on the pitch, and first basic life support was performed by paramedics when they arrived.

“He died due to an inherited heart condition, ARVC (arrhythmogenic right ventricular cardiomyopathy), that had not been identified at the time of his death.”

Staff initially believed the teen had been experiencing “a fit or seizure”, the court heard.

The inquest was told that a defibrillator was brought to the pitch within minutes of Adam’s collapse.



However, it was not used because of uncertainty around whether it was safe to apply.

The coroner added: “Agonal breathing was poorly recognised by the call handler, and it should be recognised as a feature consistent with cardiac arrest.

“The answers given to the call handler did indicate that Adam was not breathing regularly.

“She [the call handler] should have recognised either agonal breathing, cardiac arrest or abnormal breathing and should have been giving basic life support instructions.”

Agonal breathing refers to sudden, irregular gasps that are not sufficient to sustain life and require immediate CPR.



Ms Charbit said she is considering issuing a Prevention of Future Deaths report to several organisations, including the Football Association (FA), NHS England and the Department of Health and Social Care.

She will consider applications regarding her proposed report on April 14.

Adam was a student at Henley College in Oxfordshire, an aspiring footballer, and a season ticket holder of the Wanderers.

He had been part of the Henley College Elite Football Development Programme.

Following his death the teen’s dad took to social media to share a heartbreaking tribute.



Adam’s dad, Alistair, said: “Yesterday we lost my son Adam, who passed away after playing football, the sport he loved.

“We love him so much and the loss is devastating. He will always be with us and never forgotten.”

Since the teen’s tragic death, NHS England has updated the Pathways telephone triage system.

Sudden collapse during sport or exercise is now automatically treated as requiring immediate CPR.

“It must evidently be very difficult for the family to know that if Adam had collapsed after May 2025, then his collapse would have automatically been treated as a sudden cardiac arrest”, the coroner said.



In a statement after the inquest, Adam’s parents, Alastair Ankers and Naomi Wakefield, said: “We lost Adam more than two years ago, and it’s been a long and difficult process to get to this stage.

“Our family have been supported by some amazing people, for which we are truly grateful.

“Adam’s death has had a devastating impact on his family and friends.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“We hope that all the organisations and people touched by this inquest will learn and improve.

“We also thank the coroner for five proposed prevention of future death orders, and hope that no other family will have to lose a child this way.”

Football.title: Mauricio Pochettino ‘on shortlist to take over at Champions League giants’ in huge blow to Tottenham
Football.content:
MAURICIO POCHETTINO has emerged as a surprise candidate to take over European giants Real Madrid at the start of next season.

The former Tottenham and Chelsea boss currently manages the US men’s national team, and will lead them at the upcoming 2026 World Cup.





But that has not stopped Los Blancos from placing him on a shortlist of candidates for the future, according to reports from ESPN.

Real Madrid sacked former Prem star Xabi Alonso in January after nine months in charge, replacing him with reserves coach Alvaro Arbeloa.

The details of that deal, including how long it will run for, were not announced publicly.

But ESPN claim that there are hints that Arbeloa was never intended to be the long-term option.



Instead they are said to be weighing up a number of options to take over at the Santiago Bernabeu, with Pochettino personally admired by club president Florentino Perez.

The interest would represent a serious blow to Spurs as they attempt to navigate themselves out of their current crisis.

The North Londoners were heavily linked with their former manager after sacking Thomas Frank in February after the Dane dragged the side into a relegation scrap.

They eventually elected to move forward with Igor Tudor in hopes that he could right the ship at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium.



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets

But things have gotten off to a poor start for Tudor, who has lost each of his first three Premier League matches with the club.

Whether it be as another emergency replacement, or as an option for the summer should Tudor manage to keep Spurs afloat in the Prem, Pochettino has remained a popular choice among fans for the permanent job.

Chants were heard at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium calling for his appointment even before Frank was dismissed.

And that sentiment is clearly still going strong, with a group of fans seen chanting directly at the man himself about a return to the club.



Video from talkSPORT showed a number of Spurs fans chanting “He’s magic, you know, Mauricio Pochettino” after ending up on a flight with the coach on the way to watch the club’s Champions League clash with Atletico Madrid.

The Argentine can be seen smiling back and waving at the fans, who doubled down by saying “we want you back Mauricio, we want you back.”

Football.title: Newcastle vs Barcelona FREE live stream and TV guide: How to watch Champions League last-16 clash without paying a penny
Football.content:
NEWCASTLE face Barcelona TONIGHT in the first-leg of their Champions League round-of-16 tie.

The two sides last met in matchday one of this year’s league phase, in which the Magpies lost 2-1.





Marcus Rashford netted two brilliant goals that night after joining Barcelona on loan from Manchester United last summer.

Newcastle finished 12th in the group stage and qualified for the last-16 by thrashing Azerbaijani side Qarabag 9-3 on aggregate.

This year is the first time the Magpies have reached the knockout stage of the Champions League, with their best campaign previously coming in the 2002/03 season when they reached the second group stage.

Football.title: Why a Man Utd signing hasn’t played all season and is training with U21s
Football.content:
LEFT-BACK is in danger of again becoming a problem position for Manchester United.

Luke Shaw, the only United player who has started in all 30 of their Premier League games, has been forced off in the last two matches.





Patrick Dorgu is expected to be sidelined until next month with a hamstring injury and his long-term future could be as a winger.

Noussair Mazraoui, a right back by trade, also made way in the 2-1 defeat at Newcastle.

His replacement, Tyrell Malacia, emerged for only his second appearance of the season.

Mazraoui’s enforced removal in the second half cost United. Malacia allowed William Osula too much space down United’s right-hand side and then failed to close him down.



Osula whipped the ball into the far corner for a glorious 90th-minute winner.

United will release Malacia when his contract expires in the summer after four unhappy years.

The club blocked Malacia’s intended move to a Turkish club on transfer deadline day in February and he was the only member of the five-man ‘bomb squad’ who failed to secure a transfer in the summer.

Malacia, 26, missed the entirety of the 2023-24 season with a knee injury that required two rounds of surgery. He went 550 days without playing for United.



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets

Since the Dutchman’s return to the fold in November 2024, he has made ten appearances for the club.

Malacia was eventually restored to first-team training in late September.

When Malacia was named on the bench against Brighton in late-October, Shaw, Dorgu and Mazraoui were also in the squad.

Diego Leon wasn’t.

It is easy to forget that United signed a left back in the summer, in the 18-year-old Paraguayan.



Leon was actually paraded on the Old Trafford pitch alongside Matheus Cunha, Bryan Mbeumo and Benjamin Sesko before United’s final pre-season friendly with Fiorentina.

Affording Leon that privilege was clearly premature when he has not had a look-in in five months.



The Paraguayan was an unused substitute four times under Ruben Amorim but he has not made the bench since the 2-0 defeat of Sunderland on October 4.

That was Malacia’s first week back in team training.

Amorim then opted for the seniority of Malacia over the raw Leon as cover for Dorgu and Diogo Dalot, United’s left wingers when they operated in a 3-4-2-1 formation.

Leon has not been visible in any training imagery at Carrington since Christmas Eve, either.

There were murmurings he could head out on loan but instead he has been a regular for the U21s, starting in seven of their past eight games.



United believe it is more logical that Leon trains with the U21s as that is where his playing opportunities lie. He is still using first team facilities.

Amorim said during pre-season that Leon “is going to be a very good player”.

He added: “You can see it. He’s a smart guy. He’s a guy that came from Paraguay to come to the Premier League. He’s really powerful.”

It was a positive pre-season for United and Amorim probably got carried away. Leon cameoed twice against West Ham in New Jersey and Bournemouth in Chicago, the latter his last first-team minutes.

Leon started in United’s first friendly against Leeds United in Stockholm but struggled and he has not convinced at reserve level.



He showed a lack of positional awareness on his debut in Sweden, though nerves are forgivable for a rookie playing for sport’s most scrutinised team.

Playing U21 football can be a thankless task but Shea Lacey’s form earned him a promotion. Leon has never looked like returning to the fold.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Malacia became such a fixture in Amorim’s squads he was on the bench in the Portuguese’s last nine games in charge.

He has made the bench in every one of Michael Carrick’s games in the dugout.

Football.title: Tottenham played secret role in Arsenal transfer for Piero Hincapie before Gunners swooped to sign defender
Football.content:
ARSENAL hijacked Tottenham’s move for Eberechi Eze last summer – and they reportedly rushed into landing Piero Hincapie after Spurs set their sights on a deal.

The defender, 24, joined the Gunners on an initial loan from Bayer Leverkusen.





And Arsenal will trigger their £45million option to turn Hincapie’s switch into a permanent agreement at the end of the season.

But the Premier League table-toppers only swooped for the Ecuadorian when it became clear that he was on Tottenham’s list of targets.

Fabrizio Romano reports that Spurs were prepared to sanction a move which would have seen Hincapie join immediately.

But that led to Arsenal rushing into offering Leverkusen a structured agreement which would still guarantee a permanent exit from Germany.



It means that Tottenham played a secret role in the Gunners getting their hands on Hincapie, who has gone on to become boss Mikel Arteta’s first-choice left-back.

The Bundesliga title winner has made 27 appearances since his arrival at the Emirates Stadium.

And Hincapie’s form has been key in Arsenal challenging for a historic quadruple.

Fellow suitors Tottenham are desperately trying to claw themselves away from a humiliating relegation to the Championship after missing out.



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets

Thomas Frank, who was in charge of Spurs when they came calling for Hincapie’s signature, was brutally axed in February.

Igor Tudor was brought in as the Dane’s replacement until the end of the season – but he has suffered defeat in every showdown so far.

It has left Tottenham just ONE POINT adrift of the Premier League’s dreaded drop zone.

Spurs missed out on Hincapie after also being beaten by their arch-rivals in the race for Eze.

The England man, 27, snubbed Tottenham when Arsenal came calling and sealed a £67.5m move from Crystal Palace.

Football.title: Watch moment Mikel Arteta chases Declan Rice through tunnel of slaps as Arsenal prepare for Champions League clash
Football.content:
ARSENAL kicked off their Champions League last-16 preparations with a friendly round of slaps on Tuesday.

The Gunners face Bayer Leverkusen away from home in the first round of knockout games on Wednesday.





They had their final training session before flying to Germany on Tuesday.

And before things got started, Mikel Arteta jokingly chased Declan Rice through a tunnel of slaps.

Arsenal stars lined up on either side of the tunnel before hitting the midfielder on the head as he ran through.



Arteta then pushed Eberechi Eze through the tunnel.

Noni Madueke – who was celebrating his 24th birthday – and Christian Norgaard also got slapped by their team-mates ahead of the Champions League meeting.

The Gunners go into the clash with a 100 per cent win record in Europe this season.

They managed eight wins from eight in the league phase to top the table, scoring 23 goals and conceding just four.



William Saliba, Leandro Trossard and Riccardo Calafiori are expected to be available for Arsenal against Bayer Leverkusen despite injury doubts.

Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets

All three trained in front of the media with the rest of the squad on Tuesday.

Meanwhile, Martin Odegaard and Ben White were both absent and are expected to miss out, with Mikel Merino still recovering from a long-term foot injury.

David Raya, Gabriel Magalhaes, Declan Rice and Martin Zubimendi were all rested for Saturday’s trip to Mansfield Town in the FA Cup – which resulted in a 2-1 win – and will likely start.

Football.title: Trent Alexander-Arnold hits new Real Madrid low as private transfer feelings emerge
Football.content:
Trent Alexander-Arnold hits new Real Madrid low as private transfer feelings emerge

Trent Alexander-Arnold has struggled since joining Real Madrid and club chiefs appear to be far from pleased by the decision to sign him

View 2 Images Trent Alexander-Arnold has seemingly been handed another Real Madrid setback (Image: (Photo by Denis Doyle/Getty Images))

Trent Alexander-Arnold finds himself at the heart of a Real Madrid blame game following his latest setback. The former Liverpool defender has experienced a torrid beginning to life at his new club, primarily due to injury, and his displays seem to be deteriorating as a consequence.

‌



Spanish media highlighted the 27-year-old for his role in Celta Vigo's late equaliser last Friday, despite Real ultimately winning the La Liga encounter. A more detailed report from Sport has subsequently surfaced, suggesting that Los Blancos' decision-makers are all attempting to distance themselves from the Alexander-Arnold transfer.

‌



It has also been alleged that the message within the club is that the decision to recruit him was mandated by club president Florentino Perez, and that his arrival was "a direct decision by the board without consulting the manager".

‌



Liverpool told clear conditions for Yan Diomande summer transfer READ MORE :

Liverpool news: Eight stars risk European ban as two miss out on squad to face Galatasaray READ MORE :

Real's current situation at right-back has been labelled as "a serious problem" owing to Dani Carvajal's injury issues and Federico Valverde's reluctance to feature there. The report has additionally stated that the Alexander-Arnold acquisition was "exactly what Florentino likes" as he arrived from an established Premier League club on a free transfer.

His arrival has not gone to plan. Teammates Alvaro Carreras and Dean Huijsen are now being described as "managerial signings" rather than "presidential ones", with the blame game seemingly in full-effect over the deal.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Nevertheless, Alexander-Arnold will be hoping to put a difficult few months behind him with Real targeting La Liga and Champions League glory.

One positive for Alexander-Arnold is that he has been able to clock regular minutes since returning from injury in early February. He has started Real's last three matches in all competitions and will be looking to make it four this week against some familiar faces.

On Wednesday evening, Manchester City travel to the Bernabeu for the first leg of an enticing last-16 tie. The following Tuesday, the second leg takes place at Etihad Stadium.

‌



View 2 Images Trent Alexander-Arnold could be back at Anfield later this year if Real Madrid and Liverpool reach the Champions League semi-finals

The winner of that tie will face either Bayern or Atalanta in the quarter-finals. Liverpool are also on that side of the bracket and thus could face Real again after already defeating them in the league stage. Arne Slot's side must navigate a tricky tie against Galatasaray first, however.

Tottenham's trip to Atletico Madrid and Newcastle United hosting Barcelona is also scheduled for this evening, along with Bayern and Atalanta facing off in Italy.

Tomorrow's action is undoubtedly headlined by Pep Guardiola's side heading to the Spanish capital, but world champions Chelsea against holders Paris Saint-Germain is another marquee clash. Bayer Leverkusen host Arsenal in the early kick-off, with Sporting CP and Bodo/Glimt as the other last-16 tie.

Football.title: Tottenham star makes feelings clear on Arsenal tactics with brutal 'boring' dig
Football.content:
Tottenham star makes feelings clear on Arsenal tactics with brutal 'boring' dig

Tottenham forward Mathys Tel believes Premier League games are no longer entertaining to watch as more and more teams put an emphasis on set-pieces and disruptive tactics

View 2 Images Mathys Tel of Tottenham Hotspur plays during the Premier League match between Tottenham Hotspur and Crystal Palace at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in London, United Kingdom, on March 5, 2026. (Photo by Harvey Murphy/News Images/NurPhoto via Getty Images)

Mathys Tel admits the Premier League has become "boring to watch" due to some of the tactics deployed by some teams, including the current emphasis on set-pieces.

‌



The Tottenham attacker's comments relate to certain areas that Arsenal have excelled in this season, including set-pieces. The Gunners, who are on course to win their first domestic title in more than 20 years, have established themselves as the king of dead-balls having scored more goals (19) from set-pieces than any other team in the Premier League.

‌



But Tel has become the latest figure to question the quality of games in the Premier League this term after similar comments made by Ruud Gullit and Liverpool head coach, Arne Slot.

‌



“I’ll tell you the truth, yes, it’s not exciting,” Tel said on the Zack Nani Foot & Live podcast, via Maxifoot. “It’s boring to watch.

Kyle Walker announces his retirement from international football as Thomas Tuchel pays tribute READ MORE :

Declan Rice hits back at critics of Arsenal's set piece tactics with cheeky comment READ MORE :

“It’s really just a clash between two teams with their own ideas. There’s less spectacle. There’s no Vinicius pulling off a sombrero, dribbling past you, no Kylian [Mbappe] accelerating past you.

“Here, I’d say it’s more structured, maybe too much so, with clear ideas, all the set pieces, little details that can sometimes make the difference. But sometimes you think it might be too much.

‌



“I told the assistant coach in charge of set pieces: 'Don’t put me on that marking job.' Because it’s a zoo in the box.

View 2 Images Tel has hit out at the tactics that occur during set-pieces in the Premier League (Image: Getty)

“We’re all bunched together, everyone’s pushing, knocking each other to the ground, holding onto each other. Forget it. The goalkeeper can no longer get out, he can no longer see.”

‌



Arsenal have emerged as a target for criticism in recent weeks given their prowess from dead-ball situations. But Declan Rice has laughed off comments from critics like Brighton boss, Fabian Hurzeler, who took aim at the Gunners last week.

“It comes from us, the way we set up and then everyone talks about it and then everyone does it – so it’s confusing!” Rice told The i Paper.

“But they’re not doing it to the level that we do it. You can see that when you’re seeing teams take corners. We can still get better and that’s the good thing about it.”

Football.title: Man Utd star told to 'pick his battles' as Rio Ferdinand points out major flaw
Football.content:
Man Utd star told to 'pick his battles' as Rio Ferdinand points out major flaw

Manchester United defender Leny Yoro has been told to be smarter about picking his physical battles with Rio Ferdinand claiming he's being beaten too often by opposition strikers

View 2 Images Rio Ferdinand has expressed concerns about aspects of Leny Yoro's game (Image: Getty Images)

Manchester United's Leny Yoro has been told to be better at "picking his battles" by Rio Ferdinand - who thinks he's engaging in too many physical tussles.

‌



The French centre-half, still only 20, is into his second season at Old Trafford and has made himself a regular in the backline. He's had to change systems with Michael Carrick replacing Ruben Amorim, but Ferdinand believes he's leaning too much into the physical side of the game.

‌



Yoro's age profile means he still has plenty of developing to do, but he's coming up against strikers in their prime who are ready to take him on in a tussle. And Ferdinand insists he's fighting with players that he's unlikely to beat.

‌



He said on his podcast Rio Ferdinand Presents: "Leny Yoro right now is trying to fight too many fights in a game, when physically he's not starting at the same place. They're all much more physically developed. Most of the players he's playing against and No. 9s especially are stronger than him.

"So, then you've got to pick your battles. Pick your duel. Which one are you actually going to have if you've got a 60-40 in your favour? Then you go all in."

Man Utd set sights on Andoni Iraola but face competition from three Premier League rivals READ MORE :

Glazer family in £223million boost as Man Utd owners continue to see wealth soar READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Yoro has only been part of three United clean sheets in the Premier League, underlining how poorly the team have defended at times this season. The most recent of those came when he played the final 90 minutes in a 1-0 win at Everton.

The ex-Lille defender is already playing under his third manager in Manchester having been signed by Erik ten Hag. Amorim previously claimed that Yoro found it difficult to recover from setbacks during games.

He commented: "He makes a mistake in the game and then he struggles because he’s too young and he wants to do everything so well. He’s growing with games and with setbacks; it’s not easy for him as a young guy."

‌



View 2 Images Leny Yoro is still very early on in his development (Image: 2025 James Gill - Danehouse)

United's backline is now a more competitive place with Amorim gone - which has also seen the end of his back three. Previously three centre-halves were in the starting XI but Carrick has returned to a more conventional back four system. In their last game, a loss at Newcastle, Harry Maguire paired up with Yoro.

Lisandro Martinez's injury has allowed the pair to form a relationship in recent weeks, but the Argentine's return will leave any manager with a decision to make. Youngster Ayden Heaven is also highly thought of and seeking minutes to continue his development.

Football.title: Five things we learned from 10 years of Pep Guardiola at Manchester City doc
Football.content:
Five things we learned from 10 years of Pep Guardiola at Manchester City doc

Pep Guardiola has been in charge of Manchester City for a decade and his reign has included many memorable moments

View 3 Images Pep Guardiola has overseen a period of immense success at Manchester City (Image: Getty)

It has been a decade since Pep Guardiola was announced as Manchester City manager. The Catalan tactician agreed a deal with the club ahead of the 2016/17 season and joined the Premier League amid considerable scrutiny.

‌



But nearly 10 years on, he's silenced nearly - if not all of - his critics. To mark Pep Guardiola's decade in charge, All Out Football spoke with some of those who have witnessed and chronicled his era from start to finish. Here's what we discovered....

‌



Early adaptation was crucial to Pep's achievements

Beyond all the trophies, it's easy to overlook the obstacles that Guardiola has faced during his tenure. The former Barcelona and Bayern Munich boss' inaugural campaign proved somewhat disastrous, marking the first occasion in his managerial career he failed to secure any silverware.

‌



Simon Bajkowski, chief Manchester City writer for the Manchester Evening News, emphasised how rapidly Guardiola adjusted. He said: "A lot of people who wanted to see Guardiola fail in English football were absolutely delighted [after the first season]. He was swimming against the tide really. He'd made a lot of big decisions, like Claudio Bravo for Joe Hart."

FOLLOW OUR MAN CITY FB PAGE! Follow our Manchester City Mirror Football Facebook page

Adapt he did. City weren't merely Premier League champions in his second year but achieved it whilst shattering the all-time points record.

‌



Kyle Walker announces his retirement from international football as Thomas Tuchel pays tribute READ MORE :

Pep Lijnders to manage Man City against Liverpool after Pep Guardiola banned for FA Cup tie READ MORE :

Pep Guardiola is weird... in a good way

You don't remain at a club for so long, claiming all the honours Guardiola has done, without being completely dedicated to your profession. That's something Amos Murphy, journalist and co-founder of The City Ramble explained.

Speaking in the documentary, he said: "I say this in the highest esteem to a man who I hold as dear to me as a family member, but he is a weirdo in the sense he is completely absorbed by everything football. People around him say, he just doesn't stop. He can't stop."

View 3 Images Pep Guardiola's arrival at Manchester City was announced 10 years ago (Image: Getty)

‌



Broadcaster and City supporter, Ian Cheeseman, added: "There's something different about Pep, it's a love of the game, not just a love of winning."

On the psychological impact, Simon said: "You have to be a certain personality to cope with that. Some players might be able to do it for five or 10 years, some players just one or two."

Jurgen Klopp made Pep Guardiola and Man City better

When Guardiola does ultimately depart English football, among the things he'll be best remembered for are the epic clashes with Jurgen Klopp. His Liverpool side proved a constant challenge on numerous occasions, but did Klopp actually elevate Guardiola's game?

‌



City supporter Louisa Wilshaw thinks so, saying: "He did say that Klopp pushed him, made him better, made him a better manager. Made him think what he needed to do next, how he needed to improve the team."

Reflecting on the dramatic 2018/19 title race, where Liverpool's total of 97 points fell short of securing the league title, Amos said: "It was the most emotionally taxing thing I'd ever been a part of.

‌



"That 18/19 season, it was basically from September, if you drop points your chance of winning the league decreases by five per cent. You look at the points tally at the end, it turned out like that... it was the best heavyweight battle football has ever seen."

Matching Man Utd's greatest triumph

When rivals Manchester United secured the Treble in 1999, City were competing in the third tier of English football. However, in 2023, Guardiola delivered the Treble to the blue half of Manchester - something that marked a culmination for one devoted supporter.

View 3 Images Guardiola led Manchester City to a treble-winning season in 2023 (Image: Alex Livesey/Getty Images)

‌



Looking back on the 2023 success, content creator Big Steve said: "When the final whistle went, it just felt like everything had gone full circle and I'd completed what I needed to complete. From starting off as a boy at Maine Road, watching them bounce around the divisions, to watching them lift the Champions League as part of the Treble was unbelievable."

Things became even more remarkable for Steve, who ended up among the City squad's celebrations. He added: "[After the parade] things took a turn, I ended up being invited into the players' party. I was standing there with Pep, Jack Grealish was having a few beers, Ederson had no top on, Erling Haaland was walking around in pyjamas. It was an unbelievable night."

Guardiola's legacy

When Guardiola finally leaves City, it will be time to fully assess the 55-year-old's achievements. However, those associated with the club already regard him as the greatest ever.

‌



Simon commented: "History and the chance to make it is something that has really driven Pep and this team over the last 10 years. But to win four in a row, first English team to do that, I'm not sure if anyone will ever do that again."

On that accomplishment, Louisa chimed in: "I think that's awesome and that's what warrants him as the greatest of all time."

Watch the Pep Guardiola on the 10 Years Of Pep Guardiola | All Out Originals documentary on the All Out Football YouTube channel!

Football.title: Eni Aluko sends message after Joey Barton ordered to pay her £339k in court
Football.content:
Eni Aluko sends message after Joey Barton ordered to pay her £339k in court

Joey Barton has been ordered to pay £339,000 worth of damages and legal costs after losing his court battle against Eni Aluko, and now the former Lionesses star has spoken out

View 2 Images Eni Aluko gave a thumbs-up after leaving court (Image: Instagram/enialuko)

Eni Aluko has shared a message after winning her libel case against Joey Barton. Ex-footballer Barton has been told to pay £339,000 in damages and legal fees after sharing two posts about Aluko on social media platform X in 2024.

‌



The High Court heard on Tuesday that the case had been stayed, with Barton being ordered to pay 'substantial' damages and Aluko's legal costs. Barton, 43, did not attend the hearing, as barrister Gervase de Wilde told the court that he 'was arrested yesterday and is currently being held in custody'.

‌



Roughly three hours after the hearing, Aluko posted a video of her smiling and giving a thumbs-up while walking away from court on Instagram. The former Lionesses star captioned the clip: "We reached the finish line," before elaborating in a separate post.

‌



"It's been a two year marathon but we reached the finish line today," Aluko wrote. "Thank you to my brilliant legal team Judith Thompson (Samuels Solicitors), Luke Browne (5RB) and lead Counsel Gervase de Wilde (5RB) and thanks to my dear family and friends and everyone who has supported."

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Kyle Walker announces his retirement from international football as Thomas Tuchel pays tribute READ MORE :

She also told reporters outside of the London court: "I'm glad it's over." According to a court order seen by the Press Association, Barton has been ordered to pay £339,000 - with the first £100,000 plus interest due by March 24.

‌



Mr Justice Lavender granted Barton seven days to ask to change the order. Mr de Wilde, for Aluko, said that Barton had wrongly claimed that she had 'cynically sought to exploit her status as an alleged victim of racism and bullying', and that she was a hypocrite.

The barrister added that between January and August 2024, Barton published 48 posts relating to Aluko on his X account, saying that the ex-Fleetwood Town and Bristol Rovers manager had 'carried out a deliberately targeted public campaign of vilification'.

Mr de Wilde noted that one post included an image of Aluko's head superimposed onto the body of serial killer Rosemary West. "The campaign amounted to an attack on multiple aspects of her life and personality," he continued.

‌



View 2 Images Eni Aluko post (Image: Instagram/enialuko)

"Mr Barton's campaign caused Ms Aluko enormous distress. When she sought to defend herself against Mr Barton's conduct, and that of his followers, he criticised and attacked her for doing so."

Mr de Wilde went on to say that Aluko was worried that however she tried to stop the harassment, Barton 'would exploit and manipulate it to use against her'. He concluded: "Mr Barton has now accepted that his campaign against Ms Aluko amounted to harassment and that he should not have made the publications."

Football.title: Joey Barton remanded in custody over 'golf club attack'
Football.content:
Former footballer Joey Barton has been remanded in custody after being charged with assault following a fight at a golf club.



Ex-Man City and Newcastle United midfielder Barton was one of two men allegedly involved in an altercation close to Huyton and Prescot Golf Club, in Merseyside on Sunday evening. Police confirmed on Tuesday afternoon that Barton, 43, and Gary O’Grady, 50, had been charged following an alleged assault in Huyton on Sunday night.

They were charged with assault occasioning grievous bodily harm when they appeared before Liverpool magistrates. Barton, wearing glasses, a blue top and white trousers, spoke only to confirm his name and address.

Image: PA) PA)

The court heard a group of men were drinking at the bar and Barton had been in a “heated discussion” with the alleged victim, Kevin Lynch, 51. They left in opposite directions before Barton returned a few minutes later.

Chelsea Kearns, prosecuting, said soon after four males were seen outside. Two of them were taking it in turns, allegedly punching and kicking a man to the head and body. He was allegedly stamped on and kicked while on the floor. Someone was heard shouting “stop, stop”.

Image: LINKED IN/Kevin Lynch) LINKED IN/Kevin Lynch)

Some of the men left in a car as someone was heard telling one of the men he “should have behaved” the court heard. Mr Lynch was said to have suffered a “permanent, irreversible injury” and doctors fear he may lose the sight on one of his eyes.

Following the 20 minute hearing District judge James Hatton granted O’Grady conditional bail, while Barton was refused and he was remanded in custody. The men will appear at Liverpool crown court on April 7.

Mr Lynch is the head teacher at the NexGen Academy in Dovecot, an independent school set up to meet the needs of children with additional educational needs. He is known around Merseyside for his non-league footballing career, having most recently managed non-league side City of Liverpool.

Before moving into management, the 50-year-old spent time in the non-league football pyramid with the likes of Droylsden, Marine, Vauxhall Motors and Southport, where he made 36 appearances, winning the Conference in 2005.

Image: Liverpool Echo) Liverpool Echo)

After hanging his boots up, Lynch moved into coaching, notably joining Chesterfield as their first-team and senior development coach. It was in non-league across Merseyside and North Wales where Lynch remained after his playing career. He spent four years in charge of Marine, signing a two-year deal in 2010 while the club were in the seventh tier of English football.

Lynch left Marine in 2013 and took up the post at Colwyn Bay in 2015 before he was axed after a 3-0 defeat to his former club, bringing an end to his six-month stint with the Seagulls. The 50-year-old made his return to the dugout to look after Marine’s reserve team in 2020 but was soon installed as Prescot Cables manager in 2021 with the club in a relegation battle. Lynch took up the post at Valerie Park following the sacking of Craig Davies.

O'Grady has been pictured following his arrest. The LiverpoolEcho reported he has been remanded in custody.

In a statement earlier, Merseyside Police said: “We can confirm that two men have been charged following an assault in Huyton on Sunday night, 8 March.

“At around 9pm, emergency services were called to Fairway to reports of a man having been assaulted near to Huyton and Prescot Golf Club. The victim was taken to hospital for assessment of serious injuries to his face and body. He is in a serious but stable condition in hospital.”

A large police presence remained in place at the golf club on Tuesday morning as officers continued their investigation. A cordon blocking entry to the club was only removed at around midday.

Football.title: Arsenal and Liverpool transfer target responds to Premier League interest
Football.content:
Arsenal and Liverpool transfer target responds to Premier League interest

Frankfurt defender Nnamdi Collins has been linked with a Premier League switch as the likes of Arsenal and Liverpool consider the young German, who has made his stance clear

View 2 Images Nnamdi Collins is being linked with a Frankfurt exit

Frankfurt defender Nnamdi Collins admits he's "surprised" after reading rumours about a possible move to Premier League giants Liverpool or Arsenal.

‌



The 22-year-old has continued to impress in Germany, leading to the Bundesliga outfit slapping a €40million (£34m) price tag on him. Collins has become integral under new coach Albert Riera, who has made him central to his back four.

‌



Frankfurt have continued to sell their top players for the right fee - losing both Omar Marmoush and Hugo Ekitike over the past 12 months. The latter moved to Liverpool and the Reds are linked with another swoop for a player from Deutsche Bank Park.

‌



Collins himself has paid little to no attention to talk of him making a move to England with the 22-year-old, who came through the ranks at Borussia Dortmund, underlining his happiness at Frankfurt.

He told Absolut Fussball: "It actually bounces off me pretty well. I’m reading these rumours for the first time myself, which is why I’m partly surprised. I’m very happy to be here at Eintracht."

Chelsea's stance on selling Liam Delap after scoring once since summer transfer READ MORE :

Pep Lijnders to manage Man City against Liverpool after Pep Guardiola banned for FA Cup tie READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Liverpool could be on the lookout for defensive reinforcements with Ibrahima Konate still yet to sign a new deal at Anfield. His exit would leave a sizeable void whilst Joe Gomez is another defender with an uncertain future. The Reds rejigged their backline last summer, bringing in two new full-backs.

Arsenal meanwhile have a more settled backline, which is regarded as the best in Europe. The likes of William Saliba, Gabriel and Jurrien Timber are quality operators and any new defender would need to push hard for a regular starting spot.

View 2 Images Konate is out of contract at the end of the season (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Collins, capable of playing centrally and at right-back, would result in a huge profit if he did leave the club. Frankfurt have also managed to land huge profits in the past from moving on the likes of Luka Jovic, Sebastien Haller and Randal Kolo Muani with CEO Axel Hellmann explaining how they keep finding gems.

“We have one of the best coaching teams in the Bundesliga,” he told The Athletic. “That’s really important, because we sign young players and the process of educating them, which is what is making us famous at the moment, involves a lot. We are open with our strategy when we talk to the players and to other clubs.

"That’s the reason why we are well connected to other clubs, because they know we take that work seriously. We are an international club. We have the airport just around the corner. Frankfurt is an international city with 147 different nationalities. It doesn’t matter if you come from South America or Africa, you have your community here.”

Football.title: McIlroy set for late arrival at The Players after 'stubborn' back injury
Football.content:
Rory McIlroy is expected to be fit to feature in his title defence at The Players but looks set to head into the tournament without a practice round at TPC Sawgrass.

McIlroy withdrew from the Arnold Palmer Invitational ahead of his third round on Saturday, having felt "a small twinge" in his lower back during his warm-up before experiencing "muscle spasms" on the range.

The world No 2 delayed his travel to the PGA Tour's flagship event as he continued to deal with the issue, with McIlroy understood to have received treatment during Monday and Tuesday of tournament week.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player McIlroy was forced to withdraw from the Arnold Palmer Invitational with a lower back injury, moments before he was due to begin his third round

McIlroy's pre-tournament press conference - originally set for 7.45am local time (11.45am UK time) on Wednesday morning - was rescheduled, with The Masters champion now planning to travel to Ponte Vedra later that day ahead of the event starting on Thursday.

Caddie Harry Diamond has been seen walking TPC Sawgrass over the first two practice days ahead of McIlroy's arrival, with Golf Channel reporting that the five-time major winner will be on-site from Wednesday after seeing progress in his injury.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Watch McIlroy's best shots from his win at The Players in 2025

Golf Channel's Todd Lewis said: "This is a muscular issue [for McIlroy]. It is not a structural or skeletal issue, but he has been resting and receiving treatment since pulling out of the Arnold Palmer Invitational on Saturday.

"He told me yesterday (Monday) that this tweak of his lower back has been more stubborn than anticipated, so he's decided to stay home today. However, he has gotten better each and every day.

"The plan for him is to have treatment at home in South Florida, then after that treatment tomorrow morning, he's going to travel to the grounds here and start his preps for The Players.

"His intention is to participate in The Players for 2026, as the defending champion. It's likely that McIlroy will come out tomorrow and just hit balls on the player practice area, not play a nine or 18-hole practice round.

"So when he tees it up on Thursday, if everything goes as planned and his back is co-operating, he will see this golf course for the very first time."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Paul McGinley provides an insight into McIlroy's injury

The five-time major winner is scheduled to tee-off at 1.42pm local time (5.42pm UK time) on Thursday, as part of Featured Group coverage alongside two-time major winner Xander Schauffele and 2021 Masters champion Hideki Matsuyama.

He is looking to join Scottie Scheffler as the only player to defend the title at The Players, while a victory would see McIlroy join Jack Nicklaus as the only three-time winner in the tournament's history.

Image: McIlroy is looking to become a three-time winner of The Players and claim a first victory of the year

McIlroy defeated JJ Spaun in a play-off to win last year's edition in a Monday finish, six years to the day after he lifted the trophy in 2019. The Northern Irishman went on to complete the career Grand Slam a month later at The Masters.

When is The Players live on Sky Sports?

Sky Sports Golf will once again be the home of The Players, with over 40 hours of live golf across the four days and a host of extra live programming available during tournament week.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Ahead of The Players at TPC Sawgrass, we take a look at every televised hole-in-one at the iconic 17th hole

'Live from The Players' brings you the latest news from TPC Sawgrass from 1pm on Tuesday and 11.30am on Wednesday, ahead of live tournament coverage starting on Thursday.

Featured Groups will be available on Sky Sports+ throughout the week, along with Featured Holes, with bonus feeds to enjoy on top of the feeds available each week during PGA Tour coverage.

Coverage begins at 11.30am on Thursday and Friday before starting at 1pm over the weekend, with highlights and additional shows on Sky Sports Golf. Get Sky Sports or stream with no contract.

Football.title: Cheltenham Festival day two tips: 66/1 pick in the Turners!
Football.content:
The Cheltenham Festival is always a pleasure no matter what, but isn’t it great to see such big fields and competitive races at the meeting this year?

It hasn't been this competitive for quite a few years and while lovers of short-priced favs might be getting withdrawal symptoms, the opportunity to back talented horses at very big prices is what sets these big meetings apart. Day two of this year's festivities has a particularly open look to it and hopefully I can steer you in a profitable direction.

The first race of interest is the Turners' Novices' Hurdle (1.20) and the Joseph O'Brien-trained ZEUS POWER is highly likely to be the biggest-priced selection that I side with all week. The main beneficiary of the changes to the handicap rules that require horses to have had five runs over hurdles have been the Grade 1 novice hurdles, as in years gone by a horse like Zeus Power would have been teed up for the likes of the Martin Pipe Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle, but now he finds himself in this race along with 21 rivals at a huge price. However, I reckon he might well be capable of finding enough improvement to get in the mix against the odds.

The son of Protectionist has done nothing but improve since being sent over hurdles. He made it second-time lucky over obstacles when winning a maiden at Thurles by a very long way in December, impressing with his low and fluent jumping technique. For his third start he took a relative baby step up the ladder for a rated novice hurdle at Navan, but he was facing into a well-regarded sort in the Gordon Elliott-trained Lazare De Star.

Zeus Power was ridden with confidence and made fair headway to challenge up the inside, he showed plenty of bravery to see off the favourite snugly at the line. He is sure to benefit from that experience and while he would no doubt be a highly-attractive proposition in a valuable handicap hurdle off a mark of 131, fortune favours the brave and this tilt at Grade 1 novices' hurdle will hopefully reward the bravery of stepping up so steeply in class. This longer trip promises to suit him well and the style of race should also play to his strengths. He rates as a really interesting contender at a colossal price.

The Brown Advisory Novices' Chase (2.00) is another wide-open Grade 1 contest. The overpriced one in my book is the Willie Mullins-trained KAID D'AUTHIE. This is a classic case of the market not wanting to believe an unexpected result which is what Kaid D'Authie beating Final Demand at the Dublin Racing Festival was. For me, there was absolutely no fluke about it. Kaid D'Authie put Final Demand under serious pressure from a long way out and Final Demand didn't have the answers.

Image: Willie Mullins celebrated victory in the Champion Hurdle on Tuesday with Lossiemouth

It shouldn't be a huge surprise that the six-year-old has progressed as much as he has over fences as he is a huge physical specimen. There is a lot to like about his jumping technique and we may well not have seen the very best of him over fences. A sounder surface shouldn't hold any fears for him and he looks to have a great chance in a wide-open renewal of this race.

The BetMGM Queen Mother Champion Chase (4.00) has been a most catastrophic race for backers of short-price favourites in recent times. The list of apparent good things that have found a way to be beaten in this race is quite remarkable and that sums up the fine-margin nature of two-mile chasing at the highest level.

Majborough is a horse I love and hopefully a few readers of my column will have availed of the 6-1 that was available about him after he was beaten in the Hilly Way Chase, but for those that don't have such dockets in their pocket, I couldn't advise getting involved in any horse in a Champion Chase at the sort of price he is now trading at.

The one I think is of most interest at the prices is the Willie Mullins-trained IL ETAIT TEMPS. In terms of pure talent, the eight-year-old can boast form that the vast majority of this field wouldn't be capable of achieving such as beating the likes of Jonbon (twice) and Gaelic Warrior with plenty to spare.

The main stones likely to be thrown at him are that he has been beaten three times at the Cheltenham Festival and that he fell when beaten after a flat effort in the Clarence House Chase at Ascot on his latest start. Neither of those stones are overly concerning to me and I would place great significance in the fact that Mullins has removed the hood from him for this run. I expect him to roar back to top form and at his best, he would set a high bar for his rivals to come up to. At his price, I'm happy to take a chance on him.

Football.title: Lossiemouth crowned new Queen of Cheltenham with Champion Hurdle triumph
Football.content:
Lossiemouth swept aside all rivals to claim a brilliant victory in the Unibet Champion Hurdle at the Cheltenham Festival.

The 7/5 favourite, who was targeted at this race over the Mares' Hurdle - a contest she has won twice - was one of three Willie Mullins-trained runners and the choice of stable jockey Paul Townend.

The grey mare's Cheltenham record was flawless going into Tuesday's feature race and remained so afterwards, never looking in any sort of trouble ahead of a six-and-a-half-length defeat of Brighterdaysahead as The New Lion came home in third.

Alexei ran a big race in fourth, just ahead of gallant defending champion Golden Ace.

"She has that mare's pedigree which gives her that bit of speed and the cheekpieces, I think, just made a huge difference," said Mullins.

"It made her concentrate that bit more and as horses get older they probably, like the rest of us, we start looking for ways out of doing hard work and this really invigorated her.

Image: Lossiemouth leaves Brighterdaysahead in her wake in the Champion Hurdle

"When I saw her working in them the other morning and when she pulled up, Paul pulled up and I spoke to him and we both had the same feeling that this was the way to go. And then the race looked so open we thought, why not let's go for it.

"We were all anxious until the third-last and then I saw her hitting the third-last spot on and I looked back at JP's (McManus) colours (The New Lion) and I thought, right, he's starting to come under pressure and he was the one I was worried about.

"To win the Champion Hurdle definitely outranks everything else she's done. But to come back here four years in a row is an achievement in itself but then to win four years is, she's gone Triumph, Mares' twice and then this, and it's been superb.

"Just 12 months ago State Man would have left her for dead. When State Man fell last year, he might have brought her down!"

Double for McManus on 75th birthday

Image: Johnnywho returns to the winner's enclosure at Cheltenham

Saratoga and Johnnywho provided owner JP McManus with the perfect birthday presents when striking on day one at the Cheltenham Festival.

In the McCoy Contractors Juvenile Handicap Hurdle, Saratoga was one of three horses running in the green and gold silks of McManus, who was celebrating his 75th birthday, and the Padraig Roche-trained grey was a 10/1 chance under Mark Walsh.

The former Flat performer made light work of the 22-runner contest, strolling home ahead of Winston Junior to emulate his half-brother Brazil's success in 2022 for the same owner, trainer and jockey combination.

Johnnywho, who is trained by Jonjo and AJ O'Neill, just managed to sidestep Search For Glory after he unseated his rider at the last before reeling in the front-running Quebecois to win the Trustmarque Ultima Handicap Chase.

Champion trainer-elect Dan Skelton had been very bullish in the build-up to Cheltenham that Madara was his best chance of a handicap success and he truly delivered in the Sun Racing Plate Handicap Chase under Harry Skelton.

The opening day was book-ended by Henderson as Holloway Queen triumphed in the National Hunt Chase, providing James Bowen with his first Festival winner.

Football.title: Cheltenham tips: No Drama This End backed to fend off Irish challengers in Turners
Football.content:
Kate Tracey and Declan Rix return to discuss their best bets for day two of the Cheltenham Festival.

The team opened the show with a look at the Turners Novices' Hurdle which will be run over two miles and five furlongs on the old course at Prestbury Park - the first race on the card at 1.20pm.

No Drama This End heads the market, with Skylight Hustle, King Rasko Grey and Ballyfad also in situ.

Declan Rix…

"One horse who jumps well is No Drama This End. He's quite straightforward, doing an awful lot right and he's really classy. From day one the Paul Nicholls team have mentioned this horse in the same breath as Denman, which was quite brave I feel. We shouldn't forget Denman was beaten in this race by Nicanor, so hopefully it's not the same case here. I think the ground was too quick for him in the Challow, he had to go and make his own running and it wasn't a strong Challow, but there's a couple of winners that have come out of it.

"I do think softer ground if we got rain would suit this guy, and I hope the crowd gees him up because he's a bit lazy. I thought he was in trouble at Sandown and even in the Challow, a bit like Bravemansgame actually. He needs significant juice in the ground, because he does stay well. I was going to be with Doctor Steinberg, but he's going to the Albert Bartlett."

Kate Tracey…

"It's Skylight Hustle for me. He's a horse I've really liked for a long time now. He's a winner of two of his four starts under rules, to go with his point-to-point success. He's two from three over hurdles, the only defeat was at Gowran when he was second behind Thedeviluno. Since then, he has gone from strength to strength, improving rapidly to win a Fairyhouse maiden hurdle and then the Grade 1 Future Champions over two miles.

"Talk The Talk did look the winner because he headed Skylight Hustle before the last, but Skylight Hustle's jumping was sound and his attitude was great once again. He doesn't have the pace of the best two-mile novice hurdlers, so the Turners looks perfect for him."

Watch Weekend Winners in full on the At The Races YouTube channel…

Football.title: Unbridled Cheltenham day 2 preview: 66/1 fancy in the Champion Chase
Football.content:
The Queen Mother Champion Chase is the feature race on day two of the Cheltenham Festival and the Unbridled duo of Matt Chapman and Paddy Brennan have made their picks.

Willie Mullins' Majborough is the horse to beat, having impressed at the Dublin Racing Festival last month, but his jumping has been called into question by a few pundits, including Brennan.

He faces nine rivals, with the main challenge coming from Dan Skelton's L'Eau du Sud and stablemate Il Etait Temps.

Speaking on the Unbridled podcast, Brennan said: "I think Majborough needs a bit of rain. There are people out there who see him as a banker but don't forget he won on heavy ground.

"I just think L'EAU DU SUD is coming here with a better preparation, having been freshened up, and he'll love the ground.

"He's in great form at home and I'll stick with him at the prices.

"If they're riding to win, they'll have to keep an eye on Majborough, but if Willie Mullins' horse makes one mistake, I'd be jumping right upsides him.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sky Sports Racing's presenters reveal their top fancies for the 2026 Cheltenham Festival

"Majborough is his own biggest danger."

It will be no surprise to regular followers of Unbridled to learn that Chapman disagrees with his co-host.

"I think MAJBOROUGH will win but I was also wondering about LIBBERTY HUNTER at a massive 66/1. It seems to me that there's going to be a horse at a big price hitting the frame.

Image: Majborough ridden by Mark Walsh on their way to winning the Barberstown Castle Novice Chase at Punchestown

"Captain Guinness has been here before and done rather well in this race - the winner in 2024.

"If the Majborough that turns up at the Dublin Racing Festival turns up here, the race is over.

"He sometimes whacks a fence too hard but he doesn't fall."

Football.title: Barton remanded in custody after appearing in court charged with an assault
Football.content:
Joey Barton is accused of assault occasioning grievous bodily harm along with a second man after a man was seriously injured near a golf club at the weekend; the 43-year-old appeared at Liverpool Magistrates' Court on Tuesday afternoon

Joey Barton: Former footballer remanded in custody after appearing in court charged with an assault

Former footballer Joey Barton has been remanded in custody after appearing in court charged with an assault near a golf club.

The 43-year-old, from Widnes, Cheshire, is accused of assault occasioning grievous bodily harm along with a second man, Gary O'Grady, 50, from Huyton, Merseyside.

They appeared in the dock at Liverpool Magistrates' Court on Tuesday afternoon and spoke to confirm their names, addresses and dates of birth.

The men are accused of attacking Kevin Lynch at Fairway, Huyton, on Sunday.

Chelsea Kearns, prosecuting, said Mr Lynch, 51, had been at Huyton and Prescot Golf Club drinking with the two defendants on Sunday.

She said: "Footage shows Mr Lynch and Mr Barton having what seems to be a heated discussion."

The men then went outside, where a witness described seeing a man being assaulted and an ambulance was called, she said.

Mr Lynch is said to be in a serious but stable condition in hospital, but there is a possibility he may lose sight in one eye.

The case was sent to Liverpool Crown Court, where the men will appear for a plea hearing on April 7.

District Judge James Hatton told Barton, who wore a navy blue jumper, he would be remanded in custody until that hearing.

O'Grady, in a grey tracksuit, was released on conditional bail.

Earlier, at a High Court hearing on Tuesday over a libel claim involving Barton, Gervase de Wilde, representing former England footballer Eni Aluko, said his instructing solicitor had "received a call from a duty solicitor in a police station in Liverpool" shortly before the hearing.

Mr de Wilde told the court on Tuesday that Barton, who has managed Fleetwood Town and Bristol Rovers, "was arrested yesterday and is currently being held in custody".

Former Manchester City and QPR midfielder Barton has one England cap and also played for Newcastle, Rangers, Burnley and Marseille.

He now hosts podcast Common Sense With Joey Barton, which is described as an "unfiltered" look at issues in sport, society, politics and more.

Football.title: Warren: Usyk to fight Wardley-Dubois winner, then Fury | 'We're up for that!'
Football.content:
Promoter Frank Warren intends to open negotiations for Oleksandr Usyk to fight the winner of Fabio Wardley's world title clash with Daniel Dubois.

Usyk, the unified heavyweight world champion, with wins over Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua has proved himself the best in the division. Although he is now 39 years old, Usyk still plans on fighting three more times.

Next the Ukrainian will box Rico Verhoeven, a kickboxing superstar, on May 23. After that Usyk wants to fight the Wardley-Dubois winner and then have a trilogy bout with his great rival Tyson Fury.

Wardley and Dubois fight for the WBO heavyweight world championship on May 9 in Manchester and their promoter Frank Warren is open for the winner to box Usyk in a unification.

"We're up for that," Warren told Sky Sports. "There's a lot of frustration because Fabio wanted that [Usyk fight before]. But the fact that Oleksandr has come out and said he wants the winner of the two of them, it's very encouraging. And certainly for the two guys, it gives them a lot of incentive.

"We've got a great relationship with him and if we can do some business we'll certainly do it."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Fabio Wardley states he wants to fight Usyk and Tyson Fury after he has beaten Daniel Dubois

Warren also represents Fury and completing a Usyk trilogy remains a real possibility for the former champion.

"If he comes through the fight [against Wardley or Dubois], that he then wants to fight Tyson, I love that fight. The first two fights were close, very exciting fights, really exciting fights, I'd watch that all day long. And Tyson wants that fight. He's told me on numerous occasions he'd love that fight," Warren said.

For Fury fighting Usyk this year is potentially more likely than boxing Anthony Joshua.

"I don't know where AJ is, obviously we've spoken a lot about the terrible tragedy and the loss of his good friends, best friends and team members," Warren reflected.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player With Verhoeven set to fight Usyk for the WBC heavyweight title, relive when Verhoeven sparred Fury in the build-up to his triumph over Wladimir Klitschko back in 2015

"People have got to remember about the injuries that he's suffered. He's suffered physical injures but he's also suffered mental injuries in that fight. Maybe boxing, the fact of fighting will get him in a better place, I don't know.

"I don't see him fighting this year, or this year against Tyson. I may be wrong and I hope I am wrong but I don't believe he will."

The division, though, will remain active with Usyk's three-fight plan likely to come to fruition.

"This heavyweight division, as I keep saying, it's alight," Warren added. "They're great fights and we're front and centre in it. We are making the fights."

Fury: I can't beat Usyk on points

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Tyson Fury believes heavyweight king Oleksandr Usyk will be 'begging' him for a trilogy fight by the end of this year.

Fury has reiterated his desire to fight Usyk a third time, but has claimed that he will not receive a points win after losing on the scorecards in their two previous fights.

Speaking to Gareth A Davies from the Daily Telegraph, Fury said: "I want a third fight but I know if he stands up at the end of it I'm not going to get the decision, they may as well just give him the fight, I'll have the L, just give me the money.

"That's what is going to happen anyway if I don't knock him out, and if I were going to knock him out I would have done already. He's a tough man and every time he gets hurt he can nip round and run away with his speed and agility. I'm not going to get a decision, and like I said I thought I won the fights."

The 37-year-old has sparred Verhoeven in the past and believes the Dutchman has 'got a chance' of pulling off a sensational upset win.

"Before he [Rico Verhoeven] became a champion, before I became a champion, we were on the rise, hungry lions. We both went on to do a lot in the game, as champions of the world," said Fury.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Rico Verhoven showcases his hand speed ahead of world title fight against Usyk. Pictures from @ricoverhoeven.

"He's a big man, big right hand, but I'm sure Usyk has faced lots of big men with a similar style to Rico. He's got a chance, he's got power, who knows what he can do in there.

"Usyk is what, 39-years-old now, but has only had 24 fights so doesn't have a lot of mileage on the clock after turning pro in late 2013, so I think he's going to be alright."

Watch Dubois-Harper and Scotney-Flores live on Sky Sports on Sunday April 5.

Football.title: England make one change for final Six Nations match against France
Football.content:
England have made just one change to their starting line-up to face France in their final Six Nations match on Saturday as Ollie Chessum comes in for Sam Underhill.

Underhill is set to make his 50th England appearance from the bench, as is Marcus Smith.

Underhill made his debut against Argentina in 2017, while Smith first represented England in 2021 against the United States.

Coach Steve Borthwick had made major alterations for his side's historic 23-18 loss to Italy, with nine personnel changes in light of back-to-back defeats to Scotland and Ireland.

Twitter Twitter , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Twitter cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enablecookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Twitter cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Twitter cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

It marked the most changes ever made by England in the Six Nations era.

However, Borthwick is sticking to his guns this time around and the one switch sees Chessum in at blindside flanker. Tom Curry was a late withdrawal in that same position last week which saw Underhill move from the bench to starting and Chandler Cunningham-South come in as a replacement.

After starting with a win over Wales, England will be looking to avoid a fourth successive Six Nations defeat against title-hopefuls France who will be bidding to bounce back from their 50-40 loss to Scotland.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sky Sports reporter James Cole analyses what has gone wrong for England as they head into the final round of the Six Nations

"It's a huge challenge under the lights in Paris against a very strong France side," said Borthwick.

"England vs France is one of the great rivalries in international rugby and we're looking forward to the occasion."

On Underhill and Smith's milestones, Borthwick commented: "Reaching 50 caps is a special achievement and a reflection of the consistency both Sam and Marcus have shown in an England shirt.

"They have both worked extremely hard for this milestone and it's a credit to them and the people who have supported them along the way."

England team to face France

Starting XV: 15. Elliot Daly (Saracens, 75 caps), 14. Tom Roebuck (Sale Sharks, 11 caps), 13. Tommy Freeman (Northampton Saints, 26 caps), 12. Seb Atkinson (Gloucester Rugby, 3 caps), 11. Cadan Murley (Harlequins, 5 caps), 10. Fin Smith (Northampton Saints, 15 caps), 9. Ben Spencer (Bath Rugby, 17 caps), 1. Ellis Genge (Bristol Bears, 79 caps) - vice-captain, 2. Jamie George (Saracens, 109 caps) - vice-captain, 3. Joe Heyes (Leicester Tigers, 21 caps), 4. Maro Itoje (Saracens, 101 caps) - captain, 5. Alex Coles (Northampton Saints, 18 caps), 6. Ollie Chessum (Leicester Tigers, 34 caps), 7. Guy Pepper (Bath Rugby, 11 caps), 8. Ben Earl (Saracens, 50 caps)

Replacements: 16. Luke Cowan-Dickie (Sale Sharks, 57 caps), 17. Bevan Rodd (Sale Sharks, 14 caps), 18. Trevor Davison (Northampton Saints, 7 caps), 19. Chandler Cunningham-South (Harlequins, 21 caps), 20. Sam Underhill (Bath Rugby, 49 caps), 21. Henry Pollock (Northampton Saints, 9 caps), 22. Jack van Poortvliet (Leicester Tigers, 23 caps), 23. Marcus Smith (Harlequins, 49 caps)

Football.title: Galatasaray v Liverpool: Champions League last-16, first leg – live
Football.content:
now 15.11 EDT 66 min: … and now Ekitike barges his way clear down the inside-left channel, but can’t lob Cakir when one on one! The keeper flaps the ball wide left, and nothing comes of the following corner. A big chance to swing the momentum goes by. Share

1m ago 15.09 EDT 65 min: That was a very generous offside decision, because I’m not sure Yilmaz was interfering with play. Konate gets away with the dreadful backpass, and the silent-movie pratfall. Share

2m ago 15.08 EDT NO GOAL! Galatasaray 1-0 Liverpool 63 min: Liverpool escape! Yilmaz was offside earlier in the move, and Konate gets away with a huge mistake! That was beyond farcical. The big man is enduring a total nightmare. Share Updated at 15.08 EDT

3m ago 15.07 EDT GOAL! Galatasaray 2-0 Liverpool (Osimhen 62) Konate passes weakly back towards his own goal. Lang nips in. He barges down the inside-right channel and dinks into the centre. Konate, facing the wrong way and falling over, tries to backheel clear but only manages to farcically tee the ball up for Osimhen, who slams home! Share

6m ago 15.05 EDT 60 min: Yep. Frimpong comes on for Salah, while Robertson replaces Kerkez. Salah wanders off without expression. Journalists in the Ali Sami Yen prepare for some hot mixed-zone action. Share

8m ago 15.03 EDT 59 min: Arne Slot is preparing to send on Frimpong. This could be interesting. Salah has done absolutely nothing. Is he destined for the hook? Share

9m ago 15.02 EDT 57 min: Szoboszlai aims a curler towards the bottom right. Always wide. He had to take on the shot, though. There was nobody in green anywhere near the Gala box. Share

10m ago 15.01 EDT 56 min: Torreira pushes a pass down the left for Bardakcı, of all players, to break into the Liverpool box. The Galatasaray captain larrups a surprise shot into the side netting. Share

11m ago 15.00 EDT 55 min: Van Dijk is booked for clumsily shoving Yilmaz into touch. That’s both of Liverpool’s defenders on Yilmaz’s flank in the referee’s notepad. Danger here! Share

12m ago 14.59 EDT 54 min: Liverpool slow things down. Then suddenly Mac Allister looks long for Salah down the right. In the old days, it would be game over for Galatasaray. But the legs have gone. Jakobs mops up. Share

14m ago 14.57 EDT 52 min: Konate slaps a simple clearing header straight at Osimhen. And so begins a Galatasaray attack that ends with Lang curling dangerously from the left. Mamardashvili punches clear, solid goalkeeping under pressure from Osimhen. Share

15m ago 14.55 EDT 51 min: Liverpool are winning the territorial game right now. But at the risk of belabouring the point, they can’t get anything going in the final third. A lot of whistling from the home support, which is something. Share

16m ago 14.54 EDT 49 min: Gravenberch has the opportunity to release Salah into the box down the right, but clanks his pass straight into the nearest red and yellow shirt. Liverpool look fine in attack until they reach the final third, at which point the cogs begin to spin too much. Indecision reigns. Share
